This repository collects resolutions of a number of open problems from the COLT
open-problem track,
[commutative ring theory](https://www2.math.uconn.edu/~glaz/Publications_Selected%20Articles/OpenProblemsInCommutativeRingTheory.Springer14.pdf),
and an open question from a FOCS 2023 paper by the authors.

**Authors.** Binghui Peng, Runzhou Tao, Steven Wang (University of Maryland) and Hantao Yu (Columbia)

**Proof discovery.** The proofs are generated by GPT-5.5 Pro via a simple
prover–verifier pipeline; the papers are assembled with Claude Code, then
polished and verified by the authors.

**Formalization.** We also formalize the commutative ring theory solutions in
Lean using our agentic Lean formalization pipeline (linked below; source only,
`.lake` build artifacts excluded).


## Open problems answered

Each problem links to its current write-up and, where available, a
machine-checked Lean 4 formalization. Write-ups are uploaded gradually and may be
merged into a single paper over time.

### COLT open-problem track

- **Shuffled SGD — the SS–RS–GD inequalities** (Yun, Sra, Jadbabaie, COLT 2021) — [paper](papers/ss-rs-gd.pdf)
- **Unweighted data selection for linear regression** (Hanneke, Moran, Shlimovich, Yehudayoff, COLT 2025) — [paper](papers/data-selection.pdf)
- **Robust conditional probability estimation** (Langford, COLT 2010) — [paper](papers/conditional-probability.pdf)

### FOCS 2023 (authors' own open question)

- **Adversarial robustness of online leverage-score sampling** (Jiang, Peng, Weinstein, FOCS 2023) — [paper](papers/leverage-sampling.pdf)

### Commutative ring theory — Glaz et al., *Open Problems in Commutative Ring Theory*

- **Problem 4:** finite-conductor vs. quasi-coherent rings — [paper](papers/problem-4b.pdf), [Lean](lean/problem-4b-formalization/)
- **Problem 20** (Cahen–Fontana–Frisch–Glaz) — [Lean](lean/problem-20-formalization/)
- **Problem 27:** integer-valued polynomials over algebras (Werner) — [paper](papers/problem-27.pdf), [Lean (27b)](lean/problem-27b-form/)
- **Problem 30(c)** — [Lean](lean/problem-30c-formalization/)
